title: Selaginella depauperata
taxonomy: Selaginella depauperata is classified as a species of spore-producing plant in the genus Selaginella and family Selaginellaceae. It belongs to the class Lycopodiopsida and division Lycopodiophyta. This classification places it within the group of lycopods, a group of vascular plants that produce spores for reproduction. The family Selaginellaceae is a small group of plants that are often referred to as resurrection plants due to their ability to survive in a desiccated state. This unique characteristic is a result of their adaptation to arid environments.

intro: Selaginella depauperata is a type of plant belonging to the Selaginellaceae family, commonly known as spikemoss, a small, evergreen, non-vascular plant that thrives in tropical and subtropical regions. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its potential health benefits such as antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Selaginella depauperata include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Selaginella depauperata is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture to treat various health conditions, and is also used in traditional Chinese medicine as a treatment for respiratory and cardiovascular issues.
